mission.title: open-webui: Latest Node Docker image breaks build
mission.description: Latest Node Docker image breaks build. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
The latest node image breaks the build.

Latest: ` docker.io/library/node:22-alpine3.20:96cc8323e25c8cc6ddcb8b965e135cfd57846e8003ec0d7bcec16c5fd5f6d39f`

Pinning the image to the previous version still works:

`FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM

steps.title: Apply the fix for Latest Node Docker image breaks build
steps.description:
I was also facing same issue. And I looked for some explanation. Here is how it needs to be solved as I found from sources.

**********

When configuring Tailwind CSS, especially in projects using ES Modules (like those built with Vite), it's recommended to use **import instead of require**. This
```yaml
#20 13.11 ReferenceError: require is not defined
#20 13.11     at file:///app/tailwind.config.js:39:11
#20 13.11     at ModuleJobSync.runSync (node:internal/modules/esm/module_job:395:35)
#20 13.11     at ModuleLoader.importSyncForRequire (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:329:47)
#20 13.11     at loadESMFromCJS (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1414:24)
#20 13.11     at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1547:5)
#20 13.11     at Object..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1677:16)
#20 13.11     at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1318:32)
#20 13.11     at Function.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Latest Node Docker image breaks build" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I was also facing same issue. And I looked for some explanation. Here is how it needs to be solved as I found from sources.

**********

When configuring Tailwind CSS, especially in projects using ES Modules (like those built with Vite), it's recommended to use **import instead of require**.
codeSnippets:
#20 13.11 ReferenceError: require is not defined
#20 13.11     at file:///app/tailwind.config.js:39:11
#20 13.11     at ModuleJobSync.runSync (node:internal/modules/esm/module_job:395:35)
#20 13.11     at ModuleLoader.importSyncForRequire (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:329:47)
#20 13.11     at loadESMFromCJS (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1414:24)
#20 13.11     at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1547:5)
#20 13.11     at Object..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1677:16)
#20 13.11     at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1318:32)
#20 13.11     at Function._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1128:12)
#20 13.11     at TracingChannel.traceSync (node:diagnostics_channel:322:14)
#20 13.11     at wrapModuleLoad (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:219:24)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
